Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Results state the three UGT CDSs were cloned and give GenBank accession numbers 'OQ354214, OQ354222, and OQ354223'; CtUGT3 corresponds to OQ354223 in the provided ordering.

Source_Species.reason: The paper studies safflower and repeatedly identifies CtUGT3 from safflower flowers.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Title and text specify the species as 'Carthamus tinctorius L.'.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional assays in vivo used safflower corolla protoplasts, i.e., safflower.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied system was corolla protoplasts from Carthamus tinctorius L.

Enzyme_Name.reason: CtUGT3 is one of three 'UGT (UDP glycosyltransferase) genes' and the name UDP-glycosyltransferase 3 is consistent with the Results nomenclature CtUGT3.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CtUGT3 is explicitly a UGT/UDP-glycosyltransferase candidate; family 1 UGT classification is directly inferable from plant UGT nomenclature and the phylogenetic comparison with known UGTs.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: Results identify CtUGT3 among DEGs annotated to the flavonoid biosynthesis pathway and linked to flavonoid glycosides.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results and abstract conclude CtUGT3 is involved in astragalin biosynthesis and correlated mainly with flavonol 3-O/7-O glycosides.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: CtUGT3 is a glycosyltransferase acting on flavonoid aglycones/glycosides, so it functions at a downstream glycosylation step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: In vitro assays show CtUGT3 catalyzed naringenin, naringenin chalcone, apigenin, kaempferol, quercetin, isorhamnetin, and also kaempferol 7-O-glucoside.

Primary_Product.reason: Results report products including astragalin (kaempferol 3-O-glucoside), kaempferol 7-O-glucoside, kaempferol 3,7-di-O-glucoside, and other flavonoid O-glucosides.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states CtUGT3 transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to flavonoids, with 3-OH and 7-OH glycosylation activity in vitro and astragalin increase in vivo.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: CtUGT3 directly forms astragalin from kaempferol in vitro and increases astragalin in protoplast overexpression assays.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The title/abstract call CtUGT3 a 'key player' and in vivo assays support importance for astragalin formation, but the paper does not explicitly demonstrate a rate-limiting or control-step role in pathway flux.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Astragalin (kaempferol 3-O-glucoside) is the final nutrient/bioactive product directly linked to CtUGT3 in vivo.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Astragalin is explicitly named kaempferol 3-O-glucoside, a flavonol glycoside.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The abstract states flavonoid glycosides are the main bioactive components in safflower; astragalin is one such flavonoid glycoside constituent. Exact function is only broadly supported as bioactive constituent.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The study measures astragalin in safflower flowers/corolla protoplasts; thus flower corolla is supported as an accumulation site in the experimental context.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state 'the astragalin content was more than doubled when CtUGT3 was overexpressed in protoplast cells'.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in the paper: metabolome-transcriptome correlation, transient overexpression in corolla protoplasts, UPLC-QTOF-MS/MS metabolite profiling, recombinant enzyme assays, kinetic analysis, docking, and mutagenesis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered point matches Results: candidate identification by integrated omics; astragalin increase in protoplasts; broad in vitro glycosylation with 3-OH and 7-OH activity; highest catalytic efficiency for kaempferol by kcat/Km; and critical residues G15/T136/S276/E384.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results explicitly state CtUGT3 expression increased under blue light and decreased under far-red and dark conditions.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Applied species is also safflower; the same flower expression pattern is reported in Results.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The experimental treatments were different light qualities: blue, far-red, red, UV-B, white light, and dark.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Transient expression used the 'pA7-CaMV35S-YFP vector', supporting CaMV35S as the promoter.

Omics_Data.reason: The study used metabolomics and RNA-seq; raw RNA-seq data were submitted to NCBI as PRJNA831043.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a reasonable application inference from the demonstrated function and mutagenesis data, but the paper does not directly test breeding or engineering applications.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results/Discussion explicitly note that CtUGT3 has both 3-OH and 7-OH activities in vitro, but only astragalin increased significantly in vivo, suggesting context-dependent outcomes and possible competition/cooperation with other enzymes.
